scenario: Quanta Insurance runs Camunda 8.8 with 9 Spring Boot services that perform 19000 calls per hour and still sets camunda.client.zeebe.prefer-rest-over-grpc. The platform team must adopt the documented 8.8 replacement so REST routing keeps working after the next minor upgrade.
question: Which property does the 8.8 reference list as the replacement for the legacy camunda.client.zeebe.prefer-rest-over-grpc key?

options.text: The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.prefer-rest-over-grpc with camunda.client.prefer-rest-over-grpc at the unified camunda.client level in the starter.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.prefer-rest-over-grpc with camunda.client.rest-over-grpc at the unified camunda.client level in the starter.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.prefer-rest-over-grpc with camunda.client.transport.prefer-rest at the unified camunda.client level in the starter.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.prefer-rest-over-grpc with camunda.client.rest-address.prefer at the unified camunda.client level in the starter.

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client.zeebe section maps prefer-rest-over-grpc to camunda.client.prefer-rest-over-grpc; the property keeps the same name under the unified prefix.

b.text: Incorrect. The documented replacement keeps the prefer-rest-over-grpc spelling; rest-over-grpc is not listed in the 8.8 reference.

c.text: Incorrect. There is no camunda.client.transport subsection in the 8.8 reference; the property is exposed directly under camunda.client.

d.text: Incorrect. rest-address is a separate property for the REST endpoint; the prefer-rest-over-grpc replacement is a standalone boolean key.

explanation: camunda.client.zeebe.prefer-rest-over-grpc migrates to camunda.client.prefer-rest-over-grpc. The flag selects the REST gateway over gRPC for client operations and is exposed directly under camunda.client in 8.8.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
